commands.review_summary:
The PR deletes stylesheet substring tests under `ui/src/styles/**`, adds one Playwright-driven computed-style form-control browser test, and registers it in the UI Vitest node-driven browser list.

PR surface: Source +1, Tests -506. Total -505 across 12 files.

Reproducibility: not applicable. This PR is test cleanup, not a bug report with a failing user reproduction. The relevant check is whether the replacement browser test covers the retained iOS focus-zoom behavior.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Protected maintainer PR with dirty merge state and an intentional coverage tradeoff; maintainers should refresh and decide rather than queue a repair branch.

target.title: test(ui): remove stylesheet grep tests
target.body:
## Summary

This removes the raw stylesheet substring tests under `ui/src/styles/**` and keeps one replacement browser/computed-style guard for the highest-value behavior those tests covered: mobile text controls staying large enough to avoid iOS focus zoom.

The deleted tests were fragile because they asserted CSS source implementation details rather than rendered UI behavior. Most of them read stylesheet files and checked exact selector names, declaration substrings, or broad regex matches over CSS blocks. That makes harmless CSS refactors fail CI even when the UI still renders correctly, while real layout regressions can still pass as long as the expected string remains somewhere in the stylesheet.

The tradeoff here is intentionally reducing broad, shallow coverage in favor of one small rendered-style check. I kept the iOS/mobile form-control guard because it came from a concrete browser behavior and is cheap to validate with computed font sizes. I did not replace the other selector-presence/layout-string tests one-for-one because doing so recreated too much test surface for low signal, and existing browser/layout tests already cover the more important chat and sessions responsive flows.

Net effect: fewer brittle stylesheet contract tests, no production CSS changes, and a focused browser-style regression for the most user-visible behavior that was worth preserving.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/form-controls.browser.test.ts ui/src/ui/chat/chat-responsive.browser.test.ts ui/src/ui/views/sessions.browser.test.ts` - passed, browser tests skipped locally because this checkout has no Chromium executable
- `node ../node_modules/vitest/vitest.mjs run --config vitest.config.ts src/ui/form-controls.browser.test.ts src/ui/chat/chat-responsive.browser.test.ts src/ui/views/sessions.browser.test.ts` - passed, skipped locally for the same Chromium guard
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/styles --passWithNoTests` - passed, expected empty target after deleting the style grep tests
- `git diff --check` - passed
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check ui/vitest.config.ts ui/src/ui/form-controls.browser.test.ts ui/src/ui/chat/chat-responsive.browser.test.ts` - passed
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local` - clean, no accepted/actionable findings
